_comment: Proposed module -> target-package mapping for the langres architecture refactor,
transcribed from the refactor plan's target layout. Input to:
uv run python tools/import_graph.py counterfactual --mapping tools/refactor_target_packages.json
This encodes a NAIVE FILE-MOVE: every module lands whole in its target package.
It deliberately does NOT model the plan's 'contract-first' split of reports.py /
benchmark.py / _report_html.py, because that split was never specified at symbol
granularity -- see docs/research/20260716_import_graph_baseline.md, claim 5.
EXCEPTION, W1: comparator.py WAS split at symbol granularity, so it is no longer a
naive file-move. The Comparator ABC stays in langres.core.comparator -> 'core'; the
concrete StringComparator moved to langres.core.comparators -> 'components', beside
its blockers/matchers/clusterers peers. That single mis-layered module was the ONLY
backwards edge in the components <-> core knot (65 + 1): core/_exports/_blocking
imports the CONTRACT, but the contract's file was mapped to components. Splitting it
also required deleting Comparator.from_schema (the ABC's body constructed the impl --
a second, non-file-level contract -> impl dependency the plan had not accounted for);
callers now use StringComparator.from_schema, which is the identical pre-existing
factory the ABC merely delegated to.
W-1 (PRs #169/#170) added 20 modules the plan's layout had no home for. Mapped here
on the naive-file-move reading -- a facade fragment follows its parent __init__:
langres/_exports/* -> root, core/_exports/* -> core. model_ref keeps its plan-assigned
'core' home at BOTH its new path and the back-compat shim at the old one. These are
transcription decisions, not the plan's: see the doc's post-W-1 delta section.
langres._version (the stdlib-only version leaf that killed the runtime cycle) is
mapped to 'core', NOT to 'root' as the naive-file-move reading would have it. This
is a transcription decision and it is measured, not aesthetic: 'root' re-creates an
architectures <-> root knot (16 mutual pairs all-edges / 10 toplevel) because
core/resolver.py reads the version; 'core' adds no cross-package edge at all (15 / 9
-- identical to leaving it unmapped). A zero-dependency leaf read by three packages
belongs at the bottom layer; putting it in root would re-invert the exact dependency
that PR removed. Change it here if the plan says otherwise.
B1 added two modules the plan's layout had no home for, split across two packages
on purpose. langres.core.spend (the SpendMonitor/BudgetExceeded ledger, lifted out
of clients/openrouter.py) -> 'core': it is a zero-dependency leaf, like models and
usage beside it. langres.core.spend_cap (the SpendCappedMatcher enforcer) ->
'architectures', with the presets/resolver that apply it. Measured, not aesthetic:
the ledger must NOT land in a package that clients can only reach at toplevel --
openrouter re-exports both names for back-compat, and an eager re-export there is
the first toplevel clients -> core edge in the package, which drags clients into the
runtime cross-package cycle (9 -> 10). openrouter forwards them via a PEP 562
__getattr__ instead, so both views stay at their baseline (13 all-edges / 9 toplevel).
W2 moved observability out of core: runs.py, judgement_log.py and trackers/
now live in the sibling langres.tracking package (+ clients/tracking.py ->
tracking/factories.py). Every old path keeps a `# TEMPORARY: deleted by the W2
sweep` re-export shim so external `from langres.core.runs import ...` keeps
working. The shims are mapped to 'tracking' (their concern), NOT to the package
they physically sit in: mapping core.runs -> 'core' would re-create a toplevel
core -> tracking edge (the shim imports tracking.runs), the exact backwards
dependency the move removes. langres.clients.tracking needs an EXACT entry to
override the langres.clients prefix. When the W2 sweep deletes the shims, drop
these five entries (core.runs, core.judgement_log, core.trackers, and the
clients.tracking exact) with them.
The autoresearch engine and its facade are ONE target package ('optimize'),
but two source names, because `langres.optimize` is a CALLABLE: the root export
binds that attribute to the optimize() function, so any `langres.optimize.<sub>`
is unreachable by attribute traversal. The engine therefore lives in the
`langres.autoresearch` PACKAGE (-> 'prefix') and the facade stays a leaf MODULE
at langres/optimize.py (-> 'exact', like cli/eval/methods beside it -- it can
have no submodules by construction). Both still land in one package here; this
is a source-layout constraint, not a second concern.
W2 (refactor/curation) realized the 'curation' target: langres.bootstrap
dissolved into the new langres.curation PACKAGE (prefix -> 'curation', replacing
the now-dead 'langres.bootstrap' prefix), and core.{review,harvest,anchor_store,
canonicalizer} moved there (their old core.* paths survive this wave as TEMPORARY
# TEMPORARY: deleted by the W2 sweep re-export shims, still mapped -> 'curation').
langres.data.mining stays -> 'curation' as the TARGET, but this wave did NOT move
it: moving it would add a new eager langres.data -> langres.curation edge (its
functions are re-exported from langres.data) while it imports no data/ internals,
so the physical move was deferred; the plan target is unchanged.
'exact' wins over 'prefix'; among prefixes the longest match wins.
